MU.title: Nasdaq Surges Over 200 Points On Iran-Israel Ceasefire: Greed Index Remains In 'Fear' Zone

macro_events.event_summary: Trump signals an imminent Iran deal within days and a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, implying de-escalation of Middle East conflict and restoration of ~20% of global seaborne oil flow.
primary_drivers: Collapse of Middle East war risk premium in crude oil
Restoration of Strait of Hormuz shipping flows (~20mbpd transit)
Risk-on rotation away from defense/safe-haven trades

beneficiaries.rationale: Southwest is the most jet-fuel-sensitive US airline (no international hedge complexity, domestic-only, fuel ~30% of opex). A sharp drop in crude from Hormuz reopening directly expands margins.
Delta has heavy jet fuel exposure and runs its own Trainer refinery; lower crude collapses input costs and the crack spread normalization is a direct EPS tailwind.
United's long-haul international network was most exposed to Mideast airspace reroutes and fuel surcharges; de-escalation restores routes and cuts fuel cost simultaneously.
Carnival's bunker fuel costs are a major opex line and Mediterranean/Red Sea itineraries have been disrupted; deal + Hormuz reopening hits both cost and revenue headwinds at once.
Dow's ethylene/petrochemical feedstock costs fall directly with naphtha/crude; a war-premium unwind expands chemical margins on a high-operating-leverage business.
Lower diesel and gasoline prices act as a consumer stimulus and cut Walmart's own trucking/last-mile cost; classic beneficiary of falling oil at the low-end consumer.

losers.rationale: ExxonMobil is the most liquid proxy for crude price; war premium unwind plus return of Iranian barrels to market (deal implies sanctions relief) directly compresses realized prices and refining cracks.
Occidental is a high-beta US shale name (Buffett-owned, Permian-heavy) with the strongest correlation to spot WTI; falls hardest on a war-premium collapse.
APA has direct Egyptian/Suez-adjacent production and was a Mideast-tension beneficiary; deal removes both the geopolitical bid and the crude tailwind.
Lockheed has been bid on Mideast munitions demand (PAC-3, THAAD, JASSM replenishment from Israel/Gulf orders); a diplomatic resolution caps that re-order cycle narrative.
Raytheon benefits from Patriot/SM-series interceptor demand tied to active Mideast conflict; de-escalation removes the urgency premium baked into shares.
Northrop carries a defense risk-premium bid; broad defense sector unwind on peace headline, though backlog protects fundamentals (hence medium not high).
Scorpio Tankers and product tanker rates spiked on Hormuz reroute/longer ton-miles; reopening collapses the rate premium as voyages shorten back to normal.

headlines: Trump says Iran deal could be reached in 'two or three days' and Strait of Hormuz will reopen 'immediately'
